description: Evaluation schema for clinical/statistical reports against reporting guidelines (CONSORT, STROBE, PRISMA, STARD) and medical writing quality standards.

reporting_guidelines.description: Standard for reporting randomized controlled trials.

reporting_guidelines.full_name: Strengthening the Reporting of Observational Studies in Epidemiology
Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses
reporting_guidelines.description: Standard for systematic reviews and meta-analyses.

reporting_guidelines.full_name: Standards for Reporting of Diagnostic Accuracy Studies
Transparent Reporting of a multivariable prediction model for Individual Prognosis Or Diagnosis
PRISMA extension for Health Technology Assessment

acceptance_dimensions.name: reporting_guideline_compliance
acceptance_dimensions.description: Report adheres to the applicable reporting guideline checklist items.

acceptance_dimensions.name: statistical_accuracy
acceptance_dimensions.description: Statistical methods, results, and interpretations are technically correct.

acceptance_dimensions.name: clinical_relevance
acceptance_dimensions.description: Findings are interpreted in clinical context; practical implications are stated; limitations are clinically informed.

acceptance_dimensions.name: data_presentation
acceptance_dimensions.description: Tables, figures, and listings follow CONSORT/STROBE flow diagrams, present appropriate statistics, and are correctly labeled.

acceptance_dimensions.name: reproducibility
acceptance_dimensions.description: Enough methodological detail is provided for an independent statistician to reproduce the analysis.

acceptance_dimensions.name: regulatory_alignment
acceptance_dimensions.description: Report meets ICH E3/E6(R2)/E9 regulatory expectations for the applicable submission type.

acceptance_dimensions.name: writing_quality
acceptance_dimensions.description: Medical writing is clear, precise, uses standard medical terminology, and is free of ambiguity.

per_dimension_criteria.what_to_look_for: All applicable checklist items from the relevant guideline are addressed; each item is present or explicitly marked not applicable with justification.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_block: More than 3 mandatory checklist items are missing or inadequately addressed; no flow diagram provided.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_warn: 1-3 checklist items are missing or inadequately addressed; flow diagram present but incomplete.

per_dimension_criteria.what_to_look_for: Statistical methods match the research question and data type; p-values, confidence intervals, and effect sizes are correctly reported; no calculation errors; multiplicity adjustments are appropriate.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_block: Incorrect statistical method applied; calculation errors in key results; p-values inconsistent with reported test statistics; no adjustment for multiple comparisons when required.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_warn: Minor reporting omissions (e.g., missing exact p-values for non-significant results); some effect measures could be more informative.

per_dimension_criteria.what_to_look_for: Clinical significance is discussed alongside statistical significance; NNT/NNH provided where applicable; limitations acknowledge clinical generalizability.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_block: Statistical results presented without clinical interpretation; clinically irrelevant endpoints treated as primary without justification.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_warn: Clinical interpretation present but superficial; some limitations not addressed.

per_dimension_criteria.what_to_look_for: All tables and figures are referenced in text; baseline characteristics table is complete; primary endpoint results are clearly presented; CONSORT/STROBE flow diagram is accurate.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_block: Tables or figures referenced in text but missing; inconsistent numbers between text and tables.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_warn: Minor inconsistencies in table formatting; some figures could benefit from clearer labeling.

per_dimension_criteria.what_to_look_for: Analysis code or pseudocode is available; data dictionary is provided; SAP is referenced; randomization and blinding procedures are described.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_block: No code or pseudocode available; key analysis steps not described; data source not documented.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_warn: Code available but not fully annotated; some analysis steps could be described in more detail.

per_dimension_criteria.what_to_look_for: Report structure follows ICH E3 for clinical study reports; safety reporting meets ICH E2A requirements; efficacy analysis follows ICH E9.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_block: Major ICH E3 sections missing; safety data not presented per ICH E2A; primary analysis deviates from SAP without justification.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_warn: Minor ICH E3 formatting issues; some safety tables could be more complete.

per_dimension_criteria.what_to_look_for: Medical terminology is used correctly and consistently; abbreviations are defined on first use; grammar and spelling are correct.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_block: Frequent terminology errors that could cause misinterpretation; abbreviations used inconsistently.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_warn: Minor grammar or spelling issues; some terminology could be more precise.

failure_conditions.expression: two or more mandatory dimensions score 'warn'
one mandatory dimension scores 'warn' and no mandatory dimension scores 'block'
any normal-priority dimension scores 'block' and all mandatory dimensions pass
every mandatory dimension scores 'pass' and no high-priority dimension scores 'block'
